The `wait`, `notify` and `notifyAll` methods are replaced with the use of a Condition object. In this case, we can replace the `interruptWaiters` method from Java to Python by using the `condition.acquire()` method to acquire the lock associated with the condition, then calling `condition.notify_all()` to wake up all threads waiting on the condition.

Condition factors out the Object monitor
 methods (wait, notify
 and notifyAll) into distinct objects to
 give the effect of having multiple wait-sets per object, by
 combining them with the use of arbitrary Lock implementations.
 Where a Lock replaces the use of synchronized methods
 and statements, a Condition replaces the use of the Object
 monitor methods.
